sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of oranges grown from Citrus sinensis (L.) Osbeck, of the Rutaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Oranges for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of oranges grown from Citrus sinensis (L.) Osbeck, of the Rutaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Oranges for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of oranges grown from Citrus sinensis (L.) Osbeck, of the Rutaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Oranges for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
number=2
level=1

sections.title: MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS
MATURITY CRITERIA
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > MATURITY CRITERIA
number=2.2
level=2
The maturity of oranges is defined by the following parameters:
- Colouring;
- Minimum juice content, calculated in relation to the total weight of the fruit and after extraction of the juice by means of a hand press.

sections.text:
The oranges must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness account being taken of the characteristics of the variety, the time of picking and the area in which they are grown. The development and condition of the oranges must be such as to enable them:
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination. Oranges satisfying these requirements may be “degreened”. This treatment is permitted only if the other natural organoleptic characteristics are not modified.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS >
number=2.1.1
level=3

­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination. Oranges satisfying these requirements may be “degreened”. This treatment is permitted only if the other natural organoleptic characteristics are not modified.

page_spans.text:
The oranges must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness account being taken of the characteristics of the variety, the time of picking and the area in which they are grown. The development and condition of the oranges must be such as to enable them:
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination. Oranges satisfying these requirements may be “degreened”. This treatment is permitted only if the other natural organoleptic characteristics are not modified.

sections.title: Colouring
sections.text: The degree of colouring shall be such that, following normal development, the oranges reach their normal variety colour at their destination point, account being taken of the time of picking, the growing area and the duration of transport.

sections.title: Minimum Juice Content
sections.text:
- Blood oranges       30%  
- Navels group       33%  
- Other varieties       35%  
- Varieties Mosambi, Sathgudi and Pacitan  
with more than one-fifth green colour     33%  
- Other varieties with more than one-fifth green colour  45%

sections.title: CLASSIFICATION
sections.text: Oranges are classified in three classes defined below:
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > CLASSIFICATION
number=2.3
level=1
Oranges are classified in three classes defined below:

page_spans.text: Oranges are classified in three classes defined below:

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text:
Oranges in this class must be of superior quality.  In shape, external appearance, development and 
colouring, they must be characteristic of the vari ety and/or commercial type.  They must be free of defects, 
with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of 
the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > CLASSIFICATION > “Extra” Class
number=2.3.1
level=2
Oranges in this class must be of superior quality.  In shape, external appearance, development and 
colouring, they must be characteristic of the vari ety and/or commercial type.  They must be free of defects, 
with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of 
the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text:
Oranges in this class must be of superior quality.  In shape, external appearance, development and 
colouring, they must be characteristic of the vari ety and/or commercial type.  They must be free of defects, 
with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of 
the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > CLASSIFICATION > Class I
number=2.3.2
level=2
Oranges in this class must be of good quality.  They must be characteristic of the variety and/or 
commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the 
general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: 
- slight defect in shape; 
- slight defect in colouring; 
- slight skin defects occurring during the formation of the fruit, such as silver scurfs, russets,etc.; 
- slight healed defects due to a mechanical cause such as hail damage, rubbing, damage from 
handling, etc. 
 The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: Class II
PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the maximum diameter of the equatorial section of the fruit, in accordance with the following table:
Size Code Diameter (mm)
0 92 – 110
1 87 – 100
2 84 – 96
3 81 – 92
4 77 – 88
5 73 – 84
6 70 – 80
7 67 – 76
8 64 – 73
9 62 – 70
10 60 – 68
11 58 – 66
12 56 – 63
13 53 – 60
Oranges of a diameter below 53 mm are excluded.
Oranges may be packed by count. In this case, provided the size uniformity required by the Standard is retained, the size range in the package may fall outside a single size code, but within two adjacent codes.
Uniformity in size is achieved by the above mentioned size scale, unless otherwise stated, as follows:
(i) for fruit arranged in regular layers in the package, including unit consumer packages, the maximum difference between the smallest and the largest fruit, within a single size code or, in the case of oranges packed by count, within two adjacent codes, must not exceed the following maxima:
Size Code Maximum difference between fruit in the same package in mm
0 to 2 11
3 to 6 9
7 to 13 7

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
number=4
level=1
Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

page_spans.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
number=5
level=1

sections.title: QUALITY TOLERANCES
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES > QUALITY TOLERANCES
number=4.1
level=2

sections.title: SIZE TOLERANCES
sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of oranges corresponding to the size immediately above and/or below that indicated on the package. The 10% tolerance only applies to fruit whose diameter is not less than 50 mm.

PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES > SIZE TOLERANCES

sections.title: UNIFORMITY
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only oranges of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality and size, and appreciably of the same degree of ripeness and development. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents. In addition, uniformity of colouring is required for “Extra” Class.

page_spans.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only oranges of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality and size, and appreciably of the same degree of ripeness and development. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents. In addition, uniformity of colouring is required for “Extra” Class.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text: Oranges must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Oranges shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Recommended International Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995). 1 For the purposes of this Standard, this includes recycled material of food-grade quality.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Five percent by number or weight of oranges not satisfying the requirements of the class, but meeting those of Class I or, exceptionally, coming within the tolerances of that class.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: Ten percent by number or weight of oranges satisfying neither the requirements of the class nor the minimum requirements, with the exception of produce affected by rotting or any other deterioration rendering it unfit for consumption. Within this tolerance, a maximum of 5% is allowed of fruit showing slight superficial unhealed damage, dry cuts or soft and shrivelled fruit.

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the oranges. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION OF CONTAINERS
number=5.2.1
level=1
The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the oranges. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

page_spans.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the oranges. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: PRESENTATION
sections.text: The oranges may be presented as follows: (a) Arranged in regular layers in the package. This form of presentation is mandatory for “Extra” Class and optional for Classes I and II; (b) Not arranged in packages. This type of presentation is only allowed for Class I and II; (c) In individual packages for direct consumer sale of a weight less than 5 kg, either made up by number or by weight of fruit.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.retrieval_text:
CONTAMINANTS
number=7
level=1

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Recommended International Code of Practice – General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1 -1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53 -2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997).

The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997).

sentences.original: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of oranges grown from Citrus sinensis (L.) Osbeck, of the Rutaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.

sentences.original: Oranges in this class must be of superior quality.

classification.sentence: They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

sentences.original: Size is determined by the maximum diameter of the equatorial section of the fruit, in accordance with the following table:

classification.sentence:
Uniformity in size is achieved by the above mentioned size scale, unless otherwise stated, as follows:
(i) for fruit arranged in regular layers in the package, including unit consumer packages, the maximum difference between the smallest and the largest fruit, within a single size code or, in the case of oranges packed by count, within two adjacent codes, must not exceed the following maxima:
Size Code Maximum difference between fruit in the same package in mm
0 to 2 11
3 to 6 9
7 to 13 7
classification.pattern: Main obligation clause followed by conditional scope and enumerated numeric limits

components.Obligation: Size uniformity must be achieved by specified scale
components.Details: max difference in fruit size per code ranges, including packing by count

classification.justification: Obligation setting numeric limits on size uniformity with specified conditional packaging context; normative but no template match.

sentences.original: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Recommended International Code of Practice – General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1 -1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53 -2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR ORANGES (CODEX STAN 245-2004)
pages.summary: Página 1 do documento STANDARD FOR ORANGES contendo a estrutura clara de seções e subseções numeradas. Títulos principais são '1. DEFINITION OF PRODUCE' e '2. PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY' com subseções até nível 3 detalhadas. A seção 2.2.1 continua na próxima página, indicada com continues_on_next_page=true. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado e excluído do texto dos nós para qualidade em RAG.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR ORANGES
pages.summary: Página 2 do documento STANDARD FOR ORANGES contendo subseções detalhadas da seção 2. Hierarquia clara de seções 2.2.2 e 2.3 com subseções 2.3.1, 2.3.2 e 2.3.3 identificadas. O conteúdo do nó está bem associado ao texto da página. Indicação correta de continuidade do nó 2.3.3 para a próxima página. O cabeçalho foi excluído do texto dos nós para garantir qualidade no RAG.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR ORANGES
pages.summary: A página contém a seção 3 titulada 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING' com texto detalhado e tabelas associadas. A seção continua da página anterior e não segue para a próxima página. O documento e cabeçalho foram identificados corretamente e o texto na seção está limpo para uso em RAG.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 245
pages.summary: A página contém duas seções principais 4 e 5 com níveis hierárquicos secundários. A seção 4 prossegue da página anterior e inclui subseções de tolerâncias de qualidade e tamanho com níveis adicionais. A seção 5 tem subseções de uniformidade e embalagem. O texto está bem organizado e limpo para uso em RAG, com títulos claramente identificados e texto associado corretamente.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 245
pages.summary: A página contém seções 5.2.1 e 5.3 como níveis 1 com texto associado e detalhamento claro. A seção 6 inicia um novo nível 1 com subtítulos 6.1 e 6.2 no nível 2 e subdivisões de nível 3, todos com títulos e textos claros. O cabeçalho está limpo. A seção 6 prossegue da página anterior, marcado corretamente. A estrutura está adequada para RAG.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 245
pages.summary: A página 6 contém as seções 7 CONTAMINANTS e 8 HYGIENE como nível 1. A seção 7 segue da página anterior e apresenta subtópicos 7.1 e 7.2. O texto associado à seção 8 está contido completamente nesta página. Cabeçalho e rodapé foram identificados e excluídos do texto. Estrutura adequada para uso em RAG.
